title: Apple’s new Siri reaches the iOS 27 public beta
friendlyTitle: More people can now try Apple’s new Siri AI
summary: Apple has released the iOS 27 public beta, widening access to its redesigned Siri beyond developers. TechCrunch reports that the assistant can work across device information, on-screen context and web knowledge, though early beta testing has also exposed errors.
friendlySummary: Apple’s iOS 27 public beta lets more people test its new AI-powered Siri before the full launch. Early testers have also found some mistakes.
whyItMatters: A public beta is the first broad real-world test for Apple’s assistant overhaul. Its deep OS placement makes reliability, privacy and error handling consequential for everyday phone use.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Siri is built into iPhones and other Apple devices, so this test will show how useful and reliable AI help can be in daily tasks.
